compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I can migrate existing tickets' markdown file impact to structured FILE_IMPACT events via update-artifacts.sh
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend update-artifacts.sh with a data migration phase. The first registered migration (ID: file-impact-v1) scans all tickets for `## File Impact` patterns in COMMENT event bodies and description text, extracts file paths, and writes corresponding FILE_IMPACT events. Idempotent via migration stamp — re-running is a no-op.

**Why**: Backfills existing historical tickets so the structured API covers all tickets, not just newly-created ones. Prevents two-tiered data (new = structured, old = markdown).

**Scope**:
- IN: update-artifacts.sh migration phase registration, migration ID file-impact-v1 stamp mechanism, markdown pattern parser, FILE_IMPACT event writer, test suite (well-formed fixture, malformed fixture, idempotency case)
- OUT: Event type definition (Story 2985-f04d); CLI (Story 3dad-e87f); consumer refactors (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running update-artifacts.sh on a tracker containing parseable `## File Impact` patterns produces a FILE_IMPACT event for each ticket ← Satisfies: "all tickets with parseable patterns have a FILE_IMPACT event"
- When this story is complete, unparseable tickets are logged to stderr with ticket ID and skipped (not crashed) ← Satisfies: "unparseable tickets logged to stderr with ticket ID and skipped"
- When this story is complete, re-running the migration on already-migrated tickets is a no-op (stamp prevents re-run, keyed by file-impact-v1) ← Satisfies: "re-running on migrated tickets is a no-op (stamp prevents re-run, keyed by migration ID file-impact-v1)"
- When this story is complete, the test suite includes a well-formed fixture case, a malformed fixture case, and an idempotency case ← Satisfies: "Test suite includes well-formed fixture, malformed fixture, and idempotency case"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all new logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Migration stamp must be keyed by migration ID (file-impact-v1) so future migrations don't collide
- [Testing] Malformed fixture case must assert the tracker is NOT corrupted and stderr contains the offending ticket ID
- [Maintainability] The migration phase should be structured so additional migrations can be registered later without refactoring

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Migration event timestamp policy — each FILE_IMPACT event emitted by the migration MUST use the ORIGINAL ticket.created_at as its event timestamp (not migration wall-clock time). This preserves chronology so (a) the reducer latest-wins tiebreaker with any future FILE_IMPACT events correctly picks the user-written value over the migration backfill, and (b) sprint-drift-check.sh (story 2998-a1ba) sees the file_impact as pre-existing relative to children created_at and does not spuriously classify the migration itself as drift. The migration stamp file records {version: file-impact-v1, migrated_at, tickets_processed, tickets_skipped} so re-runs are idempotent by stamp AND by per-ticket last-event check. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
